title: Code Smarter With AI

modules.title: The Bug Nobody Could Find
modules.sub: Discover why AI-generated code still needs a human eye
modules.description: Learners drop into a chaotic hackathon team where an AI-written feature is silently breaking the app. They trace the bug through the code, learning why trusting output blindly is risky and why ownership of every line matters.

modules.title: Prompting for Real Code
modules.sub: Write precise prompts that get useful, usable code back
modules.description: Learners experiment with vague versus specific prompts and compare the wildly different code each produces. They practise refining their instructions — adding context, constraints, and examples — to steer the AI toward code that actually fits the project.

modules.title: Reading What AI Wrote
modules.sub: Slow down and understand code before you run it
modules.description: Learners receive a block of AI-generated code and must read through it line by line, labelling what each section does before the app is allowed to run. They build a habit of comprehension-first, discovering that understanding code and writing code are equally important skills.

modules.title: When the AI Codes It Wrong
modules.sub: Spot, explain, and fix the mistakes AI confidently makes
modules.description: Learners are handed a set of plausible-looking but flawed AI outputs — logic errors, wrong assumptions, and hallucinated functions — and must diagnose each failure. They practise correcting the code themselves and deciding when it is faster to rewrite than to repair.

modules.title: Build Something That Runs
modules.sub: Use AI as a co-pilot to ship a working feature
modules.description: Learners take on a defined feature for the hackathon app, using an AI assistant to accelerate their work while applying every skill from the earlier modules. They test their output, handle at least one AI-introduced error, and submit code they can fully explain.

modules.title: Ship It: Your AI-Assisted Project
modules.sub: Finish, reflect, and own your AI-assisted build
modules.description: Learners complete their contribution to the team app, document where AI helped and where it needed correction, and present their decisions to the group. The capstone asks them to articulate a personal workflow — when to reach for AI, when to go solo, and how to stay in control throughout.

synopsis: Teens join a student dev team racing to finish an app before a school hackathon — using AI coding assistants the whole way, but learning to read, test, and own every line. The story forces players to debug AI-generated code, prompt more precisely, and make judgment calls about when AI help is a power-up and when it is a trap.
rationale: AI coding tools like GitHub Copilot are transforming what teens can build, but without the ability to critically read and test AI-generated code, students risk shipping broken or insecure projects they do not understand.
learning_outcome: Students can use an AI coding assistant to accelerate a real project while independently reading, testing, and correcting AI-generated code — and can explain where in the workflow AI adds value versus introduces risk.
source_signals: Show HN: I quit coding years ago. AI brought me back
Show HN: PickCode – An educational coding environment for students after Scratch
Launch HN: Turing College (YC W21) – Online data science school
